does: This paper introduces the Standard Interpretable Model (SIM), a formal theoretical framework grounded in Lagrangian mechanics for systematically designing interpretable machine learning methods. The approach begins with explicit premises about what interpretability means for a target user, then deductively derives interpretability symmetries and constraints that define a Lagrangian whose minima represent optimal interpretable models. The framework enables two complementary pathways: either modifying opaque model parameters to improve interpretability or directly constructing inherently interpretable architectures by embedding constraints into the model design. The paper demonstrates empirically that SIM identifies and addresses limitations in existing interpretability approaches.
connects: This work builds on decades of interpretability research that has sought to make neural networks and complex ML models transparent and understandable, extending beyond post-hoc explanation methods like LIME and SHAP. It responds to the fragmented state of interpretability literature, where methods lack principled grounding and evaluation protocols remain inconsistent. The paper draws inspiration from physics-informed machine learning and geometric approaches to understanding model behavior. It engages with classical frameworks around model transparency, feature importance, and local explainability while bringing formal mathematical rigor through Lagrangian mechanics—a formalism previously applied to optimization and constraint satisfaction problems but novel in the interpretability context. The work synthesizes concerns from trustworthy AI, model debugging, and mechanistic interpretability.
enables: By providing a unified mathematical framework, this theory enables future work to design new interpretable methods more systematically rather than ad-hoc. Researchers can now formalize their interpretability assumptions as premises and derive corresponding methods deductively, creating a more principled landscape for innovation. The framework supports developing interpretable variants of any opaque model class and could accelerate the creation of inherently interpretable architectures tailored to specific user needs and domain constraints. This foundation potentially enables standardized evaluation protocols for interpretability by grounding assessment in the formal premises. The approach also opens possibilities for hybrid methods that balance model performance with interpretability by managing the constraint landscape, and could facilitate human-AI collaboration systems where interpretability guarantees can be formally specified and verified.
